items.note: The fuselage is called Advance+, not Advance 20

items.correct: Fatty mast for 1500+ span wings AND heavy riders
items.note: Not just for winging — it's for high-load situations

items.correct: Compare by span (rough guide) or actual CFD data
items.note: Adrian Roper: Area is meaningless across different aspect ratios. Tempo 1020 (650cm²), FB 1000 (773cm²), ART V2 999 (1024cm²) all get up the same. Only span is a rough guide.

items.correct: Surge is a different wing — better for waves/prone. Spitfire still better for CL foiling.
items.note: Luke Atkinson (Nov 2025): Spitfire better for controlled foiling (CL), Surge better for prone wave riding and winging in swell.

items.correct: Kiwi downwind mast is in prototype phase only — not released (as of Nov 2025)
items.note: Adrian Roper mentioned it exists but they don't release products until totally happy.

items.correct: PNG V2 1200 uses BLACK fuselage (unlike original PNG which used Red)
items.note: Yvon Labarthe confirmed in Apr 2025 review. Important change from PNG V1 range.

PNG.name: PNG (Pump N Glide)
PNG.tagline: World-class pump foil, legendary light-wind glide machine
PNG.character: Big span, low stall speed, not super turny but incredible glide. Best beginner and light-wind option in the AXIS lineup. Uses Red fuselage.
PNG.who_its_for: Beginners learning to foil, pump foiling athletes, downwind beginners, light-wind winging
PNG.not_ideal_for: Aggressive carving, choppy conditions, racing speed

PNG 1310.highlight: World record holder for non-stop pump foiling
PNG 1310.notes: Legendary pump foil. James Casey's choice. Best for light-wind anything.
PNG 1310.weight_range: All weights, lighter riders get more out of it

PNG 1300.highlight: James Casey's recommendation for learning downwind
PNG 1300.notes: Big span catches small wind swell. Best entry point for downwind progression. Step 1 in the downwind path.

PNG 1150.notes: Great light-wind all-rounder, very stable platform

PNG 1010.notes: Stepping stone between large and mid-range

PNG 910.notes: Great for lighter riders wanting more maneuverability

expert_quotes.quote: Start downwind on the PNG 1300 — big span catches small wind swell. It'll teach you to catch bumps.
expert_quotes.context: Downwind progression advice

expert_quotes.quote: The PNG V2 1300 blows the V1 away in speed. Consistently happy riding at 22-25 km/h, in wave riding up to 29-30 km/h. The old 1300 was slow — really slow.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS Team Rider, Nov 2024. Heavy rider on PNG V2 1300 vs V1.

expert_quotes.quote: My PNG V2 1300 setup: 82cm High Modulus Power Carbon mast, Crazy Short Advance Plus fuselage, Skinny 40 stabilizer. The crazy short gives great balance between pump frequency and carving. Psycho Short turns even better but loses pump ability.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS Team Rider, Nov 2024. PNG V2 1300 setup.

expert_quotes.quote: PNG V2 1300 control is truly a level above V1. The old 1300 sometimes felt like it had a mind of its own. That feeling is gone. V2 feels like a much smaller foil in terms of controllability.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS Team Rider, Nov 2024. PNG V2 1300 vs V1.

expert_quotes.quote: PNG V2 1300 vs Art Pro 1401: The 1401 has more glide and is easier to suck energy from. But the 1300 is more versatile and easier to use. When conditions are complicated, 1300. When water is clear, 1401. If I can only have one, it's the 1300 — it's the session saver.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS Team Rider, Nov 2024. PNG V2 1300 vs ART Pro 1401.

community_insights: PNG 1310 is the world record pump foil — it's not just marketing
Danny Perez: Went from standard to Advance+ fuselage and it made the foils so much more responsive for such large spans
Shaun Henderson: 1150 is what I learned to wing and dock start with — Advance fuselage is much better for pumping
Dominic Hoskins (80kg, Netherlands, Oct 2025): PNG V2 1200 has aspect ratio 8, PNG V2 1300 has aspect ratio 10.36. 1300 is noticeably faster/glidier.
Dominic (80kg): PNG V2 1400 is the easiest dock start foil he's ever used. Consistent, solid platform. But at 80kg can be unforgiving if balance is off — heavier riders get more control.
Dominic: PNG 1310 original — most aggressive lift in AXIS range. Best for heavyweight sub-paddle-ups. But requires pause after dock jump to stabilize before pumping. Very aggressive stall.
Dominic: Art Pro 1401 and 1201 are solid pump foils but now superseded by PNG V2 1300 and 1400 for most riders.
Dominic on ART V2: Swiss Army knife of AXIS range — does everything well. 979 and 879 his choice for messy Dutch North Sea conditions. They cut through chaos without wobble.
Dominic: Fireballs changed his life. The under-camber section created user-friendly performance. Range is incredible — can handle small micro-bumps AND huge conditions in one session.
Dominic: Fireball 1350 — dock start from static. Ultra Pro carbon mast essential for no wobble. Turn it with conviction, not hesitation.

Dominic: Tempo 1020 with Ti Link fuselage and Skinny 30 — blown away by how easy it was. Accessible to more than just elites. Not a surf foil though — kept rolling unexpectedly. Needs rider experience.
Dominic: Tempo doesn't stop rolling until you stop it. Not skittish or dangerous, but different from surf foils. Takes 10-20 minutes to adapt, then it's amazing.
Philippe Apman (70kg, AXIS team): PNG V2 1300 has AR 10.3, surface 1632cm². Best start DW wing. In wing: use for very light conditions (<10 knots at 70kg).
Olivia Piana (~65-70kg): PNG 1150 is excellent for dock start — very tolerant. Her setup: Ultrashort fuse + 375 stab.
Olivia: PNG V2 1300 glide is completely different from V1. 'Vraie évolution' (true evolution). Slightly smaller, less lift, but glide is in another class. Also turns better than V1.
DW beginner tip from Olivia: Position mast BACK on the board when starting DW. Big PNGs lift very early — too far forward = board bounces off water before you're ready.
Gamme axis positioning: BSC (easy surf/foil start) → PNG (more glide, dock start, light DW) → HPS (faster, DW progression) → ART V2 (all-around HA) → Fireball (DW/race)

BSC.name: BSC (Broad Spectrum Carve)
BSC.tagline: The best all-rounder — does everything well
BSC.character: Early pop-up, maneuverable, forgiving. Not as fast as HPS/ART but more user-friendly. True all-rounder that works for everything.
BSC.who_its_for: Beginners to intermediates wanting versatility. Anyone who does multiple disciplines on one wing.

fuselage_guide.large_BSC: Red fuselage (BSC 1060, 1120 — thicker, larger span wings)
fuselage_guide.small_BSC: Black fuselage (BSC 890 and below — thinner profile wings)

BSC 1120.notes: Best starting point for riders 90kg+. Uses Red fuselage.

BSC 1060.notes: Sweet spot for 75-90kg riders learning to wing. Uses Red fuselage.

BSC 970.notes: Under 75kg starting point. Uses Red fuselage.

BSC 890.notes: Transition to Black fuselage territory. More agile than larger BSC.

BSC 810.notes: Very popular size for intermediate riders stepping up. Excellent kite foil.

BSC 740.notes: High-wind, lighter riders, or those wanting more maneuverability

community_insights: BSC 1060 is the go-to recommendation for 75-90kg beginners — it forgives mistakes while building skills
BSC 810 is one of the most popular sizes across all riders — great stepping stone to HPS

HPS.name: HPS (High Performance Speed)
HPS.tagline: Speed + accessibility — the natural next step from BSC
HPS.character: Fast, glidey, needs more speed to get up. Natural stepping stone from BSC. Pairs well with Progressive and Speed rear wings.
HPS.who_its_for: Intermediate to advanced riders wanting speed and efficiency without going full high-aspect
warnings: HPS 880 can have pitch control challenges for some riders — if you're fighting it up/down, that's normal early on
Needs more water speed to get up compared to BSC — make sure your technique is solid first

HPS 1050.notes: Massive lift for SUP, surprisingly accessible

HPS 880.notes: Some riders report pitch control challenges initially. Use Advance fuselage and good mast for best results.

community_insights: HPS 880 pitch issues are real but manageable — give it time and focus on smooth body movements
Great stepping stone between BSC and ART — speed-focused riders love it

ART.name: ART (AXIS Research Team)
ART.tagline: Pinnacle high-aspect performance — not for choppy water
ART.character: Frictionless glide, reduced chord, high aspect. Needs Power Carbon mast and Advance fuselage for best results. NOT for turbulent water.
ART.who_its_for: Advanced winging and prone riders who have mastered HPS. Smooth water specialists.
warnings: NOT for beginners or choppy water — this is an advanced tool
ART in turbulent/choppy conditions is frustrating — Spitfire or ART V2 handles chop better
Requires Power Carbon mast for proper performance

ART 999.notes: Most popular ART size — the benchmark

community_insights: Yvon Labarthe: ART V2 1099 has 25% less glide than FB 1160 — Fireball wins for pumping
ART V2 939 turns 10-15% better than FB 1000 — better for wing/tow
ART V2 879 behind a boat in towing — turns like crazy

ART Pro.name: ART Pro
ART Pro.character: Stiffer, more responsive than standard ART. For advanced riders who can dictate the foil, not react to it.
ART Pro.who_its_for: Elite downwind racers, Kai Lenny-level riders

ART Pro 1201.highlight: Great 2nd/3rd wing for downwind progression after PNG 1300
ART Pro 1201.notes: Step 2 in downwind progression. Significantly more demanding than PNG.

ART Pro 951.highlight: Former Kai Lenny race wing (5th at M2O). He has since moved to Tempo 960.
ART Pro 951.notes: Elite racing performance. Kai Lenny raced this before switching to Tempo. Still a strong DW option for advanced lighter riders.

community_insights: Kai Lenny previously raced the ART Pro 951 (5th at M2O) before switching to the Tempo 960 for all DW racing.
Philippe Apman: ART Pro (AR ~12) — DW, free ride, long distance, GPS speed records. 2.7% camber = lighter, can pull max speed. Better Vmax than Fireball but Fireball has better average speed.
ART Pro 1201 = long-time Philippe's favorite for DW. ART Pro 1051 is the smallest he used regularly in winging (but prefers Spitfire for winging unless very light conditions).

ART V2.name: ART V2 (2024)
ART V2.tagline: More forgiving ART — Spitfire Pro character
ART V2.character: Spitfire's turn + forgiveness with ART's glide and speed. Better for UK/choppy conditions. Great intermediate downwind option.
ART V2.who_its_for: Advanced riders who want ART performance with more forgiveness. Choppy water riders.
community_insights: Yvon: ART v2 turns 5-10% better rail-to-rail but Fireball has 25% more glide — pick your priority
ART V2 879 is excellent for tow/wing, not for pumping
Better than original ART in choppy UK conditions
Dominic Hoskins (80kg): ART V2 = Swiss Army knife of AXIS range. Does everything well. 979/879 his choice for Dutch North Sea — messy, inconsistent, fast-ramping conditions.
ART V2 turns faster than equivalent span suggests — wide cord in middle gives great purchase, taper to tips allows quick roll.
ART V2 NOT surfy-feeling despite working in surf. Turns like cross between high-aspect and surf foil — everything done at pace. Very different character from Surge.
Philippe Apman: ART V2 (AR 10) = most versatile single wing if you only want one. Good for wave, surf, wing, DW. Not specialized for any discipline but competent at all.
ART V2 vs Fireball (Philippe): ART V2 slightly less glide/speed but slightly better turning than new Fireballs. More compact so easier to bank without losing support.
ART V2 characteristic: Better turning than Fireball, less glide. For mixed riders (not DW-specialist).

Spitfire.name: Spitfire
Spitfire.tagline: Wave riding specialist — sharp turns, handles turbulence
Spitfire.character: Sharp, smooth turns. Handles turbulence better than ART. Pair with Advance fuselages + small progressive rears for max carve.
Spitfire.who_its_for: Wave riders (winging, prone), choppy downwind riders, UK-style conditions

Spitfire 1180.highlight: Very popular, great for downwind progression
Spitfire 1180.notes: Step 2 in downwind progression (alongside ART Pro 1201). Handles chop better than ART.

Spitfire 1100.notes: Light wind wing, SUP, choppy downwind

Spitfire 1030.notes: Light wind wing, SUP, choppy downwind

Spitfire 960.notes: UK prone conditions, less advanced winging

Spitfire 900.notes: Extra glide vs 840. Love for smaller days and downwinding. Add Skinny rears to transform into faster DW foil.

Spitfire 840.rider_setup: Luke Atkinson (Hawaii): Advance Plus Crazy Short fuse + 250 Progressive rear
Spitfire 840.rider_feedback: Luke Atkinson: 'Turns so well, so sharp, so responsive in waves. My favorite Spitfire size.'

community_insights: Spitfire 1180 is a top choice for the 2nd wing in downwind progression — handles chop better than pure ART
Pair with Advance fuselage and small Progressive rears for maximum carve
Philippe Apman: Spitfire range 680-1180 span, AR ~8. True shortboard feel — radical rail-to-rail. Program: wave + winging for riders wanting short, radical turns. NOT for longboard-style carving.
Philippe: Spitfire in waves: radical, shortboard turns, stays up well even at small sizes. Good for learning progression and intermediate winging.

expert_quotes.quote: Spitfire 840 in waves: so sharp, so responsive, turns so well. My favorite. Paired with Advanced Plus Crazy Short fuselage and 250 Progressive rear.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS Team Rider, launch video Jun 2023. Hawaii.

expert_quotes.quote: Spitfire 900 — extra glide, perfect for smaller days and going downwind. To speed up any Spitfire, pair with skinny rears — opens up a whole new world of downwinding and glide.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS Team Rider, launch video Jun 2023.

Fireball.name: Fireball
Fireball.tagline: F1 of downwind foiling — high camber speed machine
Fireball.character: High camber (new for AXIS), ~13 AR, very low stall speed despite high performance. Gets up early, fast top end. The benchmark for SUP downwind racing.
Fireball.who_its_for: SUP downwind racers, advanced riders wanting elite DW performance, step 3 in DW progression
Fireball.fuselage: Black Series (short to psycho short for most sizes). Fatty fuse + Fatty mast for 1500/1750.

Fireball 1160.highlight: Fred Bonnet's top pick for intermediate/advanced SUP DW
Fireball 1160.notes: Fred Bonnet: 'Unbelievable, effortless.' Yvon's favorite summer all-rounder. Starts in inverted or normal in water >16°C.

Fireball 1070.notes: Yvon: Duck starts with 2 steps almost every time. Daily driver for competitive riders.

Fireball 1000.notes: Yvon: FB 1000 benchmark for turning comparison with ART V2 939

Fireball 940.notes: Dmitry Evseev's favorite (75kg): 'Needs good waves/wind but flies when conditions cooperate'

Fireball 880.notes: Smaller, faster — for advanced riders in powered conditions

Fireball 1250.notes: Yvon: FB 1250 starts 10-15% easier than FB 1160. Good light wind backup.

Fireball 1350.notes: Mark Shinn: 'I prefer the 1350 for light winds, it has a lot of control. On the 1350 I use the psycho short fuselage as it pumps so well.'

Fireball 1500.highlight: Magic pumping machine — doubles pump time vs 1350
Fireball 1500.notes: Yvon Labarthe: 'Magic! Cruising speed 17-18 km/h. You will almost double your pumping time with the 1500 compared to the 1350.' REQUIRES Fatty mast for >85kg riders. Pair with Silly Short or Psycho Short fuse and Skinny 25.
Fireball 1500.weight_range: All weights. <85kg: Ultra Pro mast OK. >85kg: MUST use Fatty mast.

Fireball 1750.notes: Yvon: 'The FB 1750 opens the hour of pumping to everyone.' Ultra Pro mast bends under load — use Fatty mast. REQUIRES Fatty mast for >85kg.
Fireball 1750.weight_range: All weights. Fatty mast required for >85kg. Ultra Pro bends under this wing.

expert_quotes.quote: I prefer the 1350 for light winds, it has a lot of control. On the 1350 I use the psycho short as it pumps so well.
expert_quotes.context: Fireball light wind recommendation

expert_quotes.quote: For the 1500 or 1750 you really need the fatty mast and fuse and they are slower...
expert_quotes.context: Fireball 1500/1750 setup requirements

expert_quotes.quote: Fireball 1160 — unbelievable, effortless.
expert_quotes.context: Top pick for intermediate/advanced SUP DW

expert_quotes.quote: FB 1500 v2 is magic! Glides straight, goes fast, turns pretty well. You will almost double your pumping time with the 1500 compared to the 1350.
expert_quotes.context: Fireball 1500 review

expert_quotes.quote: The FB 1750 opens the hour of pumping to everyone.
expert_quotes.context: Fireball 1750 review

expert_quotes.quote: I use the Fireball 1500 for pump/flat water/dock starts, paired with the 30 skinny tail. Advance Plus Ultrashort fuse.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS team rider (80kg), SurfFX interview Mar 2026.

community_insights: Fireball has 25% MORE glide than equivalent ART V2 — use Fireball for pumping, ART V2 for turning
FB 1160 is Yvon's favorite summer all-rounder — rides all boats, downwind, super long downwind
Dmitry Evseev (75kg): FB 940 needs good waves/wind but flies when conditions cooperate
Nicolas Iten did nearly 5 hours on the Fireball 1750 around a lake — ~70km. (Kai Thompson, SurfFX interview Mar 2026)
Dominic Hoskins: Fireballs are the most versatile range AXIS has produced — handles micro-bumps AND big conditions in one session. Fireballs beat ART V2 in pumping ease and range.
Dominic: Fireball 1350 can be dock-started from static. Ultra Pro Carbon mast essential. Turn with conviction not hesitation. New even-bigger sizes coming but hush-hush (as of Oct 2025).

Fireball 1500/1750 review (Meton Foil, Feb 2026): 1750 has aspect ratio 20.12, 1500 has AR 17. Paired with Skinny 25 stab and Psycho Short fuse for full efficiency. Very pitch-sensitive — needs training.

Fireball 1750 tip (Meton Foil): Use Fatty Mast for control. 1750 is NOT a beginner foil — very delicate pitch axis. Small movements needed. Nicolai Iten uses custom smaller stab for world record runs.
Fireball 1500 recommended setup: Fatty fuse (Ultrashort) + Skinny 30 rear = less pitch-sensitive than psycho-short setup. Better for most riders to start with.
Meton Foil recommendation: If choosing between 1500 and 1750 → go 1500 with psycho-short fuse + smaller tail for best efficiency/control balance.

Frank Boards/NZ rider (80-83kg, Dec 2024): Fireballs are downwind weapons. Incredibly slick, fast, good bottom end. Roll very freely — surprising for a high-aspect foil. Haven't hit stall point. Teaches you to foil — clear inputs make everything improve.
Frank Boards: Fireball rear wing sweet spot = Skinny 40. Went to 30 and 25 but 25 too cerebral — can't feel it. For racing go smaller; for DW enjoyment, 40 is perfect.
Frank Boards fuse setup for Fireballs: Short fuse (70cm Advance Plus). Going more basic gave better flow and feel. Mid-range foils don't need tiny technical fuses — they hold you back.
Frank teaser Dec 2024: 'Bigger foils coming from AXIS that are absolutely amazing — get your name on the waiting list.' (Confirmed later as Fireball 1500/1750/Tempo releases)
Frank Boards: Sam Loader interested in smallest Fireballs for towing — likes glidey foil that won't keep slowing down even at high speed in big conditions.
Philippe Apman (70kg): Fireball AR 13-14, 3.7% camber. Don't compare by area! Fireball 1000 (773cm²) performs like 900-950cm² due to high aspect. Always compare by span.
Philippe: Fireball 940 = his daily DW foil. Fireball 880 is smallest. For riders over 1000 span equivalence, move to ART Pro for bigger sizes.
Philippe: Fireball rear wing recommendations: Skinny 30 with 70cm Short fuse. Can go 25 but that's extreme. Skinny 35 is max for heavier riders with Ultrashort fuse.
Fireball vs ART Pro speed: Fireball has higher average speed (higher camber). ART Pro has better top/max speed (lighter camber 2.7%). Fireball is more gentle stall point.

design_notes.camber: High camber foil section (3.7% vs 2.5% for older ART Pro/Spitfire). Creates excellent low-speed lift but higher drag at speed. Offset by narrow span = very low drag overall.
design_notes.pump_style: Flat pump (tap tap tap) vs oscillating pump on older wings. Suits longer fuse + smaller rear. This is why Dylan uses 25 skinny with the 1070.
design_notes.straight_median_line: All Fireballs use straight median line (thickest point in straight line tip-to-tip). Makes behavior consistent at all speeds. Enables tiny rear wings.
design_notes.reflex: Hollow/reflex on underside trailing edge is NOT camber — it's reflex. Many people confuse these. Camber is the invisible line between top and bottom surfaces.

Tempo.name: Tempo
Tempo.tagline: Next-gen ultra-high-aspect DW — Ti Link required
Tempo.character: Ultra-High Modulus carbon with Ti Link titanium fuselage. Higher aspect ratios, lower volumes. Greater hydrodynamic efficiency. Carries more speed and glide than equivalent Fireball.
Tempo.who_its_for: Intermediate to advanced DW/SUP riders wanting maximum efficiency. Leucate/racing conditions.
Tempo.fuselage: ONLY 70cm fuselage available — Short Advance Plus. No choice in length currently.
Tempo.fuselage_note: Mark Shinn: 'There is only 70cm available for the Tempo. We don't have a choice in that... Sometimes Adrian makes our life simple!'

Tempo 960.highlight: Approximate equivalent to Fireball 940 but more speed/glide
Tempo 960.notes: Philippe Axman: 'Plenty of speed to win a race.' Use 30 rear wing or below.
Tempo 960.rider_feedback: Kai Lenny's current race wing — switched from ART Pro 951 to Tempo 960 for all DW racing.

Tempo 1020.notes: Good when close to shore in offshore/windy swell
Tempo 1020.rider_feedback: Kai Thompson (80kg): Go-to travel wing, covers 10-30+ knots. "Insanely overpowered at 30 and they handle it." Pairs with 360 T25 tail, 72cm UHM mast, Advance Plus Ultrashort fuse.

Tempo 1090.highlight: Biggest Tempo — nothing glides better in the world
Tempo 1090.notes: Philippe Axman: 'More glide and speed than FB [1070].' Yvon: 'Nothing glides better in the world. 15-20% less effort than FB 1070.' REQUIRES integrated tail fuselage — normal stab adapter adds crazy brake.

Tempo 890.rider_feedback: Kai Thompson (80kg): High-wind option when it's nuking.

Tempo.tempo_sizing_guide: Tempo 960 ≈ Fireball 940 | Tempo 1090 ≈ Fireball 1070 (but with more glide and speed)
Tempo.rear_wing_guide: Tempo 1090 → 30 rear (DW), 35 rear (upwind). Above 35 = gain upwind but lose glide and pumping. Tempo 960 → 30 or below.
Tempo.stab_note: Tempo rear wings are REVISED SKINNIES MONOBLOC — not standard Skinny stabs. Order specifically for Tempo.

expert_quotes.quote: Tempos carry more speed and glide vs equivalent Fireball. Tempo 960 = plenty of speed to win a race.
expert_quotes.context: Tempo vs Fireball sizing for competition

expert_quotes.quote: You need to reduce rear wing size when you go longer fuselage. Otherwise hard to pump. Longer fuse + smaller tail = less drag = faster for racing downwind.
expert_quotes.context: Fuselage length and rear wing relationship

expert_quotes.quote: Tempo 1090: nothing glides better in the world. It looks like the FB 1070 with 200cm² less. Turns better, glides better. 15-20% less effort than FB 1070.
expert_quotes.context: Tempo 1090 review

expert_quotes.quote: There is only 70cm available for the Tempo. We don't have a choice in that... Sometimes Adrian makes our life simple!
expert_quotes.context: Tempo fuselage options

expert_quotes.quote: Adrian had to redesign the entire fuselage system for the Tempos — the wing design didn't fit any existing fuselage. After Hawaii they switched from weave carbon to unicarbon all the way through, gaining 24% more stiffness.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS team rider (80kg, DW racer), SurfFX interview Mar 2026. Explaining Tempo development.

expert_quotes.quote: From the Tempos I'm getting consistency mostly. That's the biggest thing. You can be insanely overpowered on the 1020 at 30 knots and they handle it — there's no question in the riding ability because they're so stiff.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS team rider (80kg), SurfFX interview Mar 2026. On Tempo performance.

expert_quotes.quote: The Tempo 1020 is my travel wing. With 650cm² area, I can do 145-230 second paces and be comfortable everywhere. Range is insane.
expert_quotes.context: The Foil Shop Tempo & Surge Deep Dive, Nov 2025. Adrian Roper (CEO) on Tempo 1020.

expert_quotes.quote: With the Tempo, the trick is to do less — people pump like crazy on first run. Just back off and let it do its work. Very little drag, it runs free.
expert_quotes.context: The Foil Shop Tempo & Surge Deep Dive, Nov 2025. On Tempo riding technique.

expert_quotes.quote: Nine out of the top 20 at the Maui race series (M2M and M2O) were on AXIS. The Tempo is probably the dominant downwind foil setup on the market right now.
expert_quotes.context: The Foil Shop Tempo & Surge Deep Dive, Nov 2025. On racing dominance.

expert_quotes.quote: Kai Lenny started on Fireballs when he joined, then switched to the Tempo. He's run virtually every race in Hawaii, Northshore, Oahu, France — basically everywhere since then.
expert_quotes.context: The Foil Shop Tempo & Surge Deep Dive, Nov 2025.

expert_quotes.quote: Shannon's daughter Roxy learned to downwind on the Tempo 1090. First time out. That tells you everything about how easy it actually is once you're up.
expert_quotes.context: The Foil Shop, Nov 2025. On Tempo accessibility despite elite reputation.

expert_quotes.quote: Don't compare wings by area — it's meaningless. Tempo 1020 (650cm²), ART V2 999 (1024cm²), Fireball 1000 (773cm²) all get up the same. Only span is a rough guide; real comparison needs CFD.
expert_quotes.context: The Foil Shop, Nov 2025. Critical correction on wing area comparison.

community_insights: Tempo not yet available in Europe warehouse as of early 2026 — order to home country first
Tempo transition from Fireball is 'not really hard — similar DNA' (Philippe Axman)
For Leucate competition: Tempo 960 and 1090 should cover most forecasts (Philippe Axman)
Adrian Roper: 9 out of top 20 at Maui Race Series (M2M and M2O) on Axis Tempo. The dominant DW race foil currently.
Tempo 1020 is Adrian's personal travel wing — range from 145s/km to 230s/km pace. He uses it when conditions are questionable.
Tempo/Fireball tip: Do LESS pumping on first ride. The foil runs free with very little drag. Pumping like crazy fights the design.
Titanium fuselage required for Tempo because wings are so thin (<10mm at edge) that aluminum wouldn't work. Grub screw expansion plate creates rigid joint.
Tempo construction: Uni-direction carbon (no woven) on outside gives 25% more stiffness. Led to discovery for further development.
Kiwi mast (new downwind-specific mast) in development — only prototypes exist as of Nov 2025. Not yet released.
Foil Drive pocket board (AWSI 2025): Covered trench, fiberglass around battery pocket for signal transparency. Battery slides in, locked with 2 bolts. Very clean, low drag profile.
Tempo key reason for new fuselage (Adrian AWSI 2025): 16 aspect ratio wings are too thin for any existing fuselage. Titanium grub-screw expansion plate system creates rigid joint.
Olivia Piana confirmed racing Tempo 1020 (650cm² area) at 2025 Maliko Downwind race. Noted it was 'a bit too big for bumps' — average speed lower. But chosen for endurance/flat sections at end.

Surge.name: Surge
Surge.tagline: The wave weapon — back-foot surfing soul restored
Surge.character: Pure surf feel: instant response, shortboard snap, smooth rail-to-rail flow. First AXIS wing with moustache tips. Early lift despite relatively short span. Insane range from knee-high mush to outer-reef freight trains.
Surge.who_its_for: Wave riders first and foremost. Intermediate+ riders who want to shred, not just glide.
Surge.not_ideal_for: Flat water blasting (use ART V2 or Fireball instead), pure pump foiling

Surge 830.notes: Aurelien J (75kg): Very comfortable, turns well, confidence-inspiring. Uses Advance Ultrashort fuse, Skinny 360. 'Makes it feel alive and like it wants to turn.'
Surge 830.rider_feedback: Aurelien J (75kg): Very comfortable, turns well, confidence-inspiring Kai Thompson (80kg): Daily driver for surf, winging, downwind, Foil Drive. 80cm mast + Ultrashort fuse. Pairs with 345 tail.

Surge 890.notes: Luke Atkinson (100kg): 'Packs way above its weight in lift and turning. My go-to for prone, sub-paddle, parawing, winging.' Surfs harder than Spitfire 780, pumps 10x longer.
Surge 890.rider_feedback: Luke Atkinson (100kg): Go-to for everything except pure DW. Pair with 345 Skinny rear + Advance Ultrashort fuse.
Surge 890.recommended_rear: Surf Skinny 280-345 depending on conditions. Luke uses 300 for surf, 345 for general

Surge 950.rider_feedback: Kai Thompson (80kg): Best for dock starting competition. Won Boot Düsseldorf dock start event using this surf wing against dedicated race/starting foils.

Surge 1010.highlight: Mark Shinn's 'Best AXIS foil I've ridden to date'
Surge 1010.notes: Best dock start pump wing per multiple team riders. Great for people learning to downwind (easy control, good pump). Kai, Luke, Gray Morris all rode 1010 in Croisic dock start.
Surge 1010.rider_feedback: Mark Shinn: 'Best AXIS foil I've ridden to date'
Surge 1010.weight_range: Works well for heavier riders too — Gray Morris (100kg) made finals at Croisic dock start
Surge 1010.future: Larger sizes (1080, 1150) in development per Nov 2025 hint

Surge.parawing_setup: Jerome recommends Skinny Surf 280 rear wing for parawing with Surge

expert_quotes.quote: Best AXIS foil I've ridden to date.
expert_quotes.context: After testing the Surge 1010

expert_quotes.quote: The Surge is designed to RIP waves and be easy to pump back out too.
expert_quotes.context: Surge design intent

expert_quotes.quote: The Surge 830 is my go-to for surf, winging, downwind, Foil Drive — I take it everywhere. The 830 and 950 cover me for everything including dock starts.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS team rider (80kg), SurfFX interview Mar 2026. On Surge versatility.

expert_quotes.quote: I used the Surge 950 for dock starting comp at Boot Düsseldorf — beat guys running dedicated starting foils with a surf wing. Made a few people think differently.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS team rider (80kg), SurfFX interview Mar 2026. On Surge in dock start competition.

expert_quotes.quote: If you're foil driving and have Axis, the Surge is awesome — really nice efficient glide cruising without the drag. Some surf setups are more draggy and less pumpy. This has way more freedom.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS team rider (80kg), SurfFX interview Mar 2026. On Surge with Foil Drive.

expert_quotes.quote: The Surge is a pure surf wing by design — but it happens to be really good for dock start pump, power wing, and even learning to downwind in bigger sizes. It does everything.
expert_quotes.context: The Foil Shop Tempo & Surge Deep Dive, Nov 2025

expert_quotes.quote: With the Surge, the key innovation is a flat middle section then turn-down at the tips — not a full arc. Full arc locks into turns. Ours lets you surf freely, engage a bottom turn, and whack the lip. Free, not locked.
expert_quotes.context: The Foil Shop Tempo & Surge Deep Dive, Nov 2025. On moustache/dihedral design.

expert_quotes.quote: At 100kg I ride the 890 and it packs way above its weight in terms of lift and turning. My go-to for prone, sub-paddle, parawing, winging — pretty much anything. The 830 is next as my main prone wing.
expert_quotes.context: The Foil Shop, Nov 2025. Luke Atkinson, AXIS team rider, ~100kg

expert_quotes.quote: Coming off my 780 Spitfire, I can surf the 890 Surge harder AND pump it 10 times longer. I've never looked back.
expert_quotes.context: The Foil Shop, Nov 2025. On transitioning from Spitfire to Surge.

expert_quotes.quote: The 1010 works fantastic for dock start — it enters the water really well because of the moustache tips. Even Yvon Labarthe said it was the best wing he'd ever used for engaging from a dock start.
expert_quotes.context: The Foil Shop, Nov 2025. On dock start competition in Croisic/Crozon.

expert_quotes.quote: From the moment I got on it, the foil said — is that it? I can do more than that. You end up rolling harder every turn trying to find the limit. And I haven't found it yet.
expert_quotes.context: Adrian + Mark Shinn on Surge, Nov 2025. On Surge roll and turn character.

expert_quotes.quote: The Surge resolves exactly what I always wanted from the Fireball — the glide and pump are there, but now you can make aggressive turns. It pumps like the Fireball — you can feel the Fireball in the Surge.
expert_quotes.context: Adrian + Mark Shinn on Surge, Nov 2025.

community_insights: Aurelien J (75kg) on Surge 830: Very comfortable, turns well, confidence-inspiring. Advance Ultrashort fuse + Skinny 360.
Jerome: Skinny Surf 280 is the go-to rear wing for parawing with Surge
Mark Shinn: 'Best AXIS foil I've ridden to date' — for the Surge 1010

Riders transitioning from ART V2 find they don't need as big a Surge as expected. The forward drive (from Fireball DNA) is the #1 unexpected selling point — not just the roll/turn feel. (SurfFX / Kai Thompson, Mar 2026)
Luke Atkinson (100kg) on Surge 890: Surfs harder than Spitfire 780 and pumps 10x longer. All-around do-everything wing.
Luke Atkinson: Surge 1010 was best dock start wing — even Yvon Labarthe said it was the best he'd tried for dock start engagement.

Surge rear wing recommendation: Luke uses Surf Skinny 300 as main surf skinny — pumps better than progressives, turns better than regular skinnies. At 100kg, 280 felt insufficient pump, 320 felt too stiff.
Gray Morris (100kg): Also rode the 1010 in Croisic dock start event and made the finals.
Adrian: Surge tip design — flat middle section then turn-down tip (not full arc) = free surfing feel, no locked turns.
Surge is NOT a replacement for Spitfire — Spitfire better for CL foiling. Surge better for prone, wave surfing, winging in swell.
Surge 890 at 100kg (Luke): use with 345 rear skinny, Advance Ultrashort fuse as go-to setup.

Christian (Wing Server, Feb 2026): Surge A890 fits perfectly between Spitfire and ART V2. Very forgiving and stable in messy water — can continuously adjust foil angle without stalling. Not as fast as ART V2 but similar glide.

Christian: Surge excels when pushed in hard turns using energy from bumps and swell. Better pop for jumping than Spitfire at same size. Still need to size correctly — 890 may be big if coming from Spitfire 780.
Christian: When to choose ART V2 879 over Surge: fast moving waves, can keep up with pocket. Generates way more speed. But Surge is looser and more forgiving on smaller/messier days.
Christian: For overhead powerful waves where you don't need glide, Spitfire 780 still best. In those conditions, going too big on any foil = getting spit out.
Christian: ART V2 879 is standout size. On smaller days, switch to A939 or 999 — they glide better at lower speeds.

Christian: Fireball 1250 = amazing light wind downwind/power wing foil. Prefers smooth drawn-out turns, not snappy. Very high aspect foils don't lock in well in lots of water movement when winging upwind.
Size comparison: Surge A890 more forgiving than Spitfire 780 despite bigger span. Moving from ART V2 triple9 → recommend Surge 890 or 950. Moving from ART V2 819 → Surge 830.

PNG V2.name: PNG V2
PNG V2.tagline: Upgraded pump-and-glide — faster, better control than original PNG
PNG V2.character: Same great lift as PNG but noticeably more speed, better control, and better glide. Same foil section as Fireball for excellent low-speed lift.
PNG V2.who_its_for: All weights needing pump/dock start/light wind capability. Heavier riders' go-to (Mark Shinn's 'session saver').
PNG V2.fuselage: Black Series (unlike original PNG which uses Red)

PNG V2 1200.notes: Yvon Labarthe (75kg): For complete beginners >85kg up to 120kg. Goes on BLACK fuselage (unlike original PNG). 120cm span, very maneuverable. Lots of lift at low speed, not fast. Heavier riders (95-100kg+) make it go faster. If <85kg with experience → PNG V2 1300 is better.
PNG V2 1200.rider_feedback: Yvon (75kg): Tried it in small waves — turns very nicely. Can do some DW. Perfect beginner wing for heavier riders.

PNG V2 1300.notes: Aspect ratio 10.36. Speed: 22-25 km/h regular, 29-30 km/h in waves. Significantly faster than V1. Best versatility/control balance. First wing in Mark's bag on any trip.
PNG V2 1300.rider_setup: Mark Shinn: 82cm HM Power Carbon mast, Crazy Short Advance Plus fuse, Skinny 40 stabilizer
PNG V2 1300.rider_feedback: Mark Shinn: 'V2 feels like a much smaller foil in terms of controllability. The mind-of-its-own feeling is gone.' V1 comparison: V2 is faster, better glide, better control, same pump or slightly better.

PNG V2 1400.highlight: Yvon's winter pump favorite — 7-8 minutes vs 3 min on Fireball 1350
PNG V2 1400.notes: Yvon Labarthe (75kg): 140cm span, cruises 13.8-13.9 km/h. HUGE surprise — pumps 7-8 min without going anaerobic vs only 3 min on Fireball 1350. Heart under 160 BPM. Recovers ease-of-use from PNG V1 1300. Patrick (112kg) gets 15-16 km/h on it.
PNG V2 1400.weight_range: All weights — heavier riders especially love it
PNG V2 1400.rider_feedback: Yvon: Recovered that easy feeling from PNG V1 1300 — which PNG V2 1300 lost in pursuit of more speed/glide. Best winter pump foil for those who love pumping long.

expert_quotes.quote: The PNG V2 1300 blows the V1 away in speed. Consistently happy riding at 22-25 km/h, in wave riding up to 29-30 km/h. The old 1300 was slow — really slow.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS Team Rider, Nov 2024. Heavy rider on PNG V2 1300 vs V1.

expert_quotes.quote: My PNG V2 1300 setup: 82cm High Modulus Power Carbon mast, Crazy Short Advance Plus fuselage, Skinny 40 stabilizer. The crazy short gives great balance between pump frequency and carving. Psycho Short turns even better but loses pump ability.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS Team Rider, Nov 2024. PNG V2 1300 setup.

expert_quotes.quote: PNG V2 1300 control is truly a level above V1. The old 1300 sometimes felt like it had a mind of its own. That feeling is gone. V2 feels like a much smaller foil in terms of controllability.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS Team Rider, Nov 2024. PNG V2 1300 vs V1.

expert_quotes.quote: PNG V2 1300 vs Art Pro 1401: The 1401 has more glide and is easier to suck energy from. But the 1300 is more versatile and easier to use. When conditions are complicated, 1300. When water is clear, 1401. If I can only have one, it's the 1300 — it's the session saver.
expert_quotes.context: AXIS Team Rider, Nov 2024. PNG V2 1300 vs ART Pro 1401.

expert_quotes.quote: PNG V2 1200 — for complete beginners 85-120kg. Goes on BLACK fuselage. Super maneuverable at 120cm span. Very beginner, lots of lift at low speed. If under 85kg with experience, take the V2 1300 instead.
expert_quotes.context: French YouTube review, Apr 2025. Yvon Labarthe (75kg).

expert_quotes.quote: PNG V2 1400 — a big surprise! With Fireball 1350 in winter (water at 6°C), I only last 3 minutes. With the 1400 I easily pump 7-8 minutes without going over 160 BPM. Very easy, not demanding. Patrick at 112kg gets 15-16 km/h on it.
expert_quotes.context: French YouTube review, Apr 2025. Yvon Labarthe (75kg).

fuselage_guide.overview: AXIS fuselages come in Red and Black series. Red for larger/thicker wings (BSC, PNG, Spitfire). Black for thinner/performance wings (ART, HPS, Fireball, Tempo, Surge).

red_series.description: 3/4 block aluminum, 4x 8mm bolts in T-formation. Strongest connection on market.
red_series.note: Essential for big span wings — strongest connection

sizes.Standard: Best for riders 240lb+ or largest wings
sizes.Short: Best for riders ~180lb, most common Red option

black_series.description: Thinner head, 2x 8mm + 2x 6mm bolts. Less drag for performance wings.

sizes.Ultrashort: Most popular intermediate/advanced — sweet spot of maneuverability and stability
sizes.Psycho Short: Extreme pump — used by Mark Shinn with Fireball 1350

advance_plus.description: Mast position 60mm further forward vs standard fuselage.
advance_plus.use_case: Advanced surf performance, tight turns on waves
advance_plus.best_with: ART wings, Spitfire, for maximum carve
advance_plus.note: NOT called 'Advance 20' — the correct name is 'Advance+'

ti_link.description: Aerospace-grade titanium, machined to absolute precision.
ti_link.notes: Designed specifically for Tempo but compatible with all AXIS setups. Premium option for ultimate performance.

fatty_fuse.description: Heavy-duty fuselage for 1500+ span wings and heavy riders.
fatty_fuse.use_case: Fireball 1500, Fireball 1750, heavy riders (85kg+) pushing limits
fatty_fuse.note: Mark Shinn: 'For the 1500 or 1750 you really need the fatty mast and fuse'

principles.longer_fuse: More stability, easier for beginners, better for big wings
principles.shorter_fuse: More maneuverable, advanced riders, tighter turns
principles.popular_choice: Ultrashort is the most popular intermediate/advanced fuselage length
principles.rear_wing_relationship: Philippe Axman: Longer fuse requires smaller rear wing to maintain pump efficiency. Longer fuse + smaller tail = same pitch stability + faster racing speed.

19mm_aluminum.name: 19mm Aluminum
19mm_aluminum.character: Gold standard, stiffest aluminum on market
19mm_aluminum.who_its_for: All disciplines, essential for larger span wings. Best all-around choice.
19mm_aluminum.notes: Most reliable, most durable. Start here.

power_carbon.name: Power Carbon
power_carbon.who_its_for: Advanced riders, big guys, biggest wings, those pushing limits
power_carbon.notes: Essential for ART series to get proper performance. 'More positive feel with immediate response'
power_carbon.when_to_upgrade: When you feel flex limiting your performance, or riding ART series

ultra_pro_carbon.name: Ultra Pro / PRO Ultra High Modulus Carbon
ultra_pro_carbon.who_its_for: Elite performance, when you want the absolute best
ultra_pro_carbon.notes: Yvon Labarthe: Ultra Pro slides super well but bends under Fireball 1750 load. Use Fatty mast for 1500+.
ultra_pro_carbon.cost: Outrageously expensive — for those who must have the best

fatty_mast.name: Power Carbon FATTY / Fatty Mast
fatty_mast.character: Extra torsional stiffness for extreme loads
fatty_mast.who_its_for: 1500+ span wings (Fireball 1500, 1750) AND heavy riders (85kg+) pushing limits
fatty_mast.critical_note: REQUIRED for Fireball 1500/1750 if rider is >85kg. Ultra Pro mast bends under 1750. Yvon confirmed.
fatty_mast.common_misconception: Not just for 'winging' — for high-load situations regardless of discipline

carbon_integrated_foil_drive.name: Carbon Integrated Foil Drive
carbon_integrated_foil_drive.notes: Special mast accommodating Foil Drive unit. Battery sits in board cutout.

notes: Fatty mast system: 95mm profile mast cut down — fatter from base all the way through. MUST pair with fatty fuselage (wider mounting). Recommended for heavier riders (80kg+) on Fireballs. Lighter riders (sub-80kg) can use standard mast. (Kai Thompson / SurfFX, Mar 2026)

Fatty mast stiffness test (Adrian Roper, Nov 2025): 900mm mast, 25kg side load. 19mm Alum: 38mm bend. Power Carbon: ~29mm. Power Carbon HM: ~23mm. Ultra Pro: ~19mm. FATTY MAST: 11mm. Nearly DOUBLE the stiffness of Ultra Pro. Required for 1500/1750 span wings.
Mast thickness impacts wing selection (Adrian): 1750 span = fatty mast. Under 1350 span at <90kg = Ultra Pro. Mix-match at your own peril.
New Kiwi mast for DW coming (Adrian, Nov 2025) — only 11mm through bottom, absolute min drag for Tempo system under 90kg. Not released yet.

steps.description: Learn to catch bumps. Big span, forgiving, catches small wind swell. James Casey's recommendation for DW beginners.
Step up to higher aspect. ART Pro 1201 for speed-focused, Spitfire 1180 for those wanting chop forgiveness.
Elite downwind performance. Fireball 1160 = Fred Bonnet's top pick. ART Pro 951 = Kai Lenny's race wing.
The next level. Ultra-high-aspect, Ti Link fuselage, maximum efficiency. For serious competitors.

Alternative to PNG 1300 for beginners who want wave/surf capability. Easy to control, early lift, great pump. Yvon Labarthe: 'Best wing for dock start engagement.' Bigger sizes coming for heavier rider DW beginners.

beginner_winging_80kg.title: Beginner Winging — ~80kg Rider
beginner_winging_80kg.notes: Most forgiving setup. Focus on getting up and controlling altitude before worrying about performance.

beginner_winging_heavy.title: Beginner Winging — 90kg+ Rider
beginner_winging_heavy.notes: Extra lift and stability for heavier riders. Red Standard fuselage essential for this weight.

intermediate_allround.title: Intermediate All-Round
intermediate_allround.notes: Speed and versatility. HPS 880 requires good body mechanics to manage pitch.

advanced_winging_prone.title: Advanced Winging/Prone
advanced_winging_prone.notes: Maximum performance. ART needs Power Carbon to perform properly.

advanced_surf.title: Advanced Surf (Wave Riding)
advanced_surf.notes: Surge is purpose-built for waves. Jerome recommends Surf Skinny 280 for parawing specifically.

downwind_beginner.title: Downwind — Beginner
downwind_beginner.notes: Big span to catch small bumps. Don't rush to smaller wings — stay here until you catch bumps consistently.

downwind_intermediate.title: Downwind — Intermediate
downwind_intermediate.notes: Fred Bonnet: FB 1160 is 'unbelievable, effortless' for intermediate/advanced SUP DW.

downwind_advanced.title: Downwind — Advanced/Racing
downwind_advanced.fuselage: Ti Link 70cm (Tempo) or Black Psycho Short (Fireball)
downwind_advanced.mast: Ultra Pro Carbon or Power Carbon
downwind_advanced.notes: Yvon: Tempo 1090 = 15-20% less effort than FB 1070. Philippe: Tempo 960 and 1090 cover most Leucate race forecasts.

pump_foiling_endurance.title: Pump Foiling / Endurance
pump_foiling_endurance.mast: Fatty mast for 1500/1750. Ultra Pro if <85kg and using 1500.
pump_foiling_endurance.notes: Yvon: FB 1750 opens the hour of pumping to everyone. Silly Short or Psycho Short fuselage for maximum pump efficiency.

Fireball.text: FB 1500 v2 is magic! You will almost double your pumping time with the 1500 compared to the 1350. Cruising speed 17-18 km/h.
The FB 1750 opens the hour of pumping to everyone.
FB 1160: I rode it all summer, it's my favorite foil for riding. Did almost all boats, downwind, super long downwind.
Fireball 1160 — unbelievable, effortless. Top pick for intermediate/advanced SUP downwind.
FB 940 is my favorite — needs good waves/wind but flies when conditions cooperate.

Tempo.text: Tempo 1090: nothing glides better in the world. It looks like the FB 1070 with 200cm² less. 15-20% less effort than FB 1070.
Tempo 960 = plenty of speed to win a race. Tempos carry more speed and glide than equivalent Fireball.
Tempo 920 was my Maui race wing. Never felt I needed anything bigger for the races. Tried the 890 — wish I'd used that for all of them. Speed and carry is insane.
For France races I use the Tempo 1020. That's my wing when conditions are questionable — range from 145s/km to 230s/km.

Surge.text: Best AXIS foil I've ridden to date. The Surge is designed to RIP waves and be easy to pump back out.
Surge 830: Very comfortable, turns well, confidence-inspiring. Uses Advance Ultrashort fuse, Skinny 360.
Surge 890 is my do-everything wing at 100kg. Surfs harder than Spitfire 780, pumps 10x longer. The 830 will be my next main prone wing.
Surge 1010 with 890 ultra short fuse and 345 rear — Grey Morris and I both used for dock start competition at Croisic. Made finals. Incredibly reliable for dock start.

PNG.text: Learned on PNG 1310 & 1300. Went from standard to Advanced+ fuselage and it made the foils so much more responsive for such large spans.
PNG V2 1300 is my session saver — first wing into my bag on any trip. 82cm HM Power Carbon mast, Crazy Short Advance Plus fuse, Skinny 40 stabilizer. Consistently 22-25 km/h, up to 30 km/h in waves.
PNG V2 1400 — 7-8 minutes pump in winter vs 3 minutes on Fireball 1350. Heart under 160 BPM. Cruises 13.8-13.9 km/h. Not as fast but WAY more endurance. Perfect winter pump foil.

ART V2.text: With ART V2 1099, I have 25% less glide than FB 1160 — Fireball wins for pumping. ART v2 turns 5-10% better rail-to-rail.
ART v2 879 behind a boat in towing — ça tourne grave! (turns like crazy). For wing, everyone loves ART v2.

common_pitfalls.description: Some riders struggle with pitch control on the HPS 880 — over-correcting up/down. This is normal early on. Focus on smooth, gradual body movements rather than reactive corrections.
common_pitfalls.solution: Give it time. Use Advance fuselage and proper mast. Most riders get past this within 5-10 sessions.

common_pitfalls.description: Standard ART wings struggle in turbulent/choppy conditions — they're designed for smooth water. Using ART in chop leads to frustration.
common_pitfalls.solution: Use ART V2 or Spitfire for choppy conditions. Save ART for smooth water days.

common_pitfalls.description: Using a Black fuselage with PNG or large BSC (or Red fuselage with ART/Fireball). Each fuselage type is engineered for specific wing geometries.
common_pitfalls.solution: Red for PNG/BSC/large Spitfire. Black for HPS/ART/Fireball/Tempo/Surge.

common_pitfalls.description: Yvon Labarthe confirmed the Ultra Pro mast bends significantly under the Fireball 1750. If you're over 85kg, this is unsafe.
common_pitfalls.solution: Use Fatty mast for Fireball 1500/1750 if you're >85kg.

common_pitfalls.issue: Tempo with wrong rear wing adapter
common_pitfalls.description: Using a normal stab adapter with Skinny rear wings on Tempo adds significant drag ('crazy brake' — Yvon).
common_pitfalls.solution: MUST use Tempo's integrated tail fuselage. Order Tempo-specific revised Skinny monobloc stabs.

common_pitfalls.description: Moving to high-aspect wings (ART Pro, Fireball) before consistently catching bumps on PNG 1300.
common_pitfalls.solution: Stay on PNG 1300 until you consistently link bumps. Then step to Spitfire 1180 or ART Pro 1201.

common_pitfalls.description: Original ART series (V1) struggles significantly in choppy water with tidal currents. Aggressive stall, excessive front foot pressure. Only 899 size works reasonably well in chop.
common_pitfalls.solution: Switch to ART V2 for choppy conditions — designed with these issues fixed. Spitfire also better in chop than ART V1.

nfc_authentication.description: AXIS now embeds NFC tags in wings (announced Nov 2025 in Foil Shop interview).
nfc_authentication.how_it_works: Remove phone case, tap NFC tag on wing. Opens page with serial number, SKU, EIN, security code to verify genuine product.
nfc_authentication.purpose: Combat Chinese counterfeiting. Tag contains: serial number, SKU, product info link, registration portal.
nfc_authentication.location: On the fiberglass isolation barrier of the wing
nfc_authentication.note: Adrian Roper: 'We've had quite a bit of copying from Chinese companies building fake Axis stuff. This helps stop that.' Plan to add to all products going forward.
nfc_authentication.source: The Foil Shop Tempo & Surge Deep Dive, Nov 2025

reasons_riders_leave.title: Why Riders Switch Away from AXIS
sources: YouTube: 'Why I changed from Axis to Duotone' - Dec 2024

issues.issue: Original ART V1 choppy water performance
issues.detail: ART V1 wings don't like turbulent water with tidal currents. Aggressive stall at low speed. Excessive front foot pressure at speed. 899 was the only size that worked well in chop.
issues.resolved_by: ART V2 addressed these issues significantly

issues.issue: ART Pro aggressive stall / hard to get up
issues.detail: ART Pro 1051 harder to get going than ART V1, more aggressive stall. Not fun to turn in choppy water.
issues.resolved_by: Fireball and ART V2 have more forgiving behavior

issues.detail: AXIS fuselage beds down INTO the wing (making it thinner), requiring more expensive ultra-HM carbon to maintain strength. Customer pays premium for manufacturing constraint not design benefit.
issues.status: Ongoing structural concern — accurate for older wings

issues.issue: Perception of brand direction (prone/DW focused, not winging)
issues.detail: Long-time winger felt AXIS direction shifted heavily toward downwind/prone/pump in 2023-2024 period, less focus on pure winging performance.
issues.status: Partially addressed with Surge (2025) and continued ART V2 development

issues.issue: Ultra-HM Mast pricing for small foil riders
issues.detail: Tapered carbon mast (£3000) aimed at large-span DW foils. No cheaper tapered mast option for winging with smaller span wings.
issues.status: Valid concern — AXIS mast lineup focuses on DW performance

reasons_riders_leave.advisory_note: These concerns are valuable for understanding AXIS weaknesses vs competitors. The ART V2 (2024) and Surge (2025) address wave performance issues. Pricing and mast range remain valid concerns for some riders.

Duotone.Glide V2: Full plane foil — smoother entry feeling. Rider Ash switched to Duotone Glide 2.0 from AXIS HPS/ART range. Found Duotone HA foils more playful, better low end, no aggressive stall vs AXIS ART V1.
Duotone.Whiz: Like a Spitfire with better low end and more carve capability. Lacks speed vs AXIS.
Duotone.SLS Mast: Tapered mast at ~£1300 vs AXIS Ultra-HM at ~£3000. Significant price advantage.

North.MA V2 1050: Fantastically forgiving and playful mid-aspect. No ton of glide but very easy to use.
North.HM Mast: New HM tapered mast — well-received in market

AK.Tracer 1030: Full plane foil — smooth riding. Axis doesn't offer full-plane connection for front wings.

Luke Atkinson.role: AXIS Team Rider, Foil Drive Team Rider

known_setups.wave_winging: Surge 890, 345 Skinny rear, Advance Ultrashort fuse
known_setups.spitfire_era: Spitfire 840, Advance Plus Crazy Short fuse, 250 Progressive rear
known_setups.foil_drive: AXIS prone board 29L/43cm, 90cm mast, prototype surge-family wing ~900-1000 span

key_facts: 95kg at time of Foil Drive podcast (Jun 2025)
Won Foil Fest comp at Point Plumber, Australia
Former kite surfing national titles winner
Luke's Surge 890 surfs harder than his old Spitfire 780 and pumps 10x longer
Made finals at Croisic dock start with Surge 1010 (alongside Gray Morris and Kai Thompson, all ~100kg)

Olivia Piana.role: AXIS Europe Development, Former World Record DW holder
Olivia Piana.background: 10 years elite SUP racing. Switched post-COVID to winging → SUP foil → DW foiling. Holds/held M2M women's record.

known_setups.maliko_race_2025: Tempo 1020 (650cm² area) — confirmed in Jul 2025 Maliko race video
known_setups.preferred: Smaller foils for bumpy conditions; bigger (Tempo 1020) for flat sections/endurance

key_facts: First employee in AXIS Europe development role (as of early 2025)
One of very few women in foiling industry leadership
Reports to Evan and Adrian at AXIS
Fireball is the top-selling AXIS product in Europe per her Jul 2025 report
Has done Maliko multiple times — prefers bumpy over flat conditions
Described AXIS as 'one of the brands that makes the best gear'
Friends and races with Frank Maffei, Thiago, Nick at Maliko

Frank Maffei.role: Frank Boards shaper, DW foil evangelist, AXIS user

known_setups.fireball_dw: Fireball (various sizes) + Skinny 40 rear + Short Advance Plus fuse (70cm) + 72cm Pro mast
known_setups.fuse_philosophy: Short (70cm) for Fireballs/mid-range. Silly Short (56cm) for bigger foils. Crazy Short (60cm) for smaller.

key_facts: Shaped boards used by AXIS twins (Perth, etc.) and Pace
Present at Maliko races with AXIS team
Friends with Olivia Piana, Thiago (meets at Maliko)
Helped sand/prepare AXIS masts in Maui for Tempo race system
Teased 'incredible bigger foils coming' Dec 2024 (pre-Tempo/1500/1750 release)

europe_2025.top_demand: Fireball series — biggest demand across Europe
europe_2025.notes: AXIS expanding in Europe — lots of work needed for brand awareness. 60+ front wings is confusing for customers.
europe_2025.pump_foiling: Growing in Europe especially landlocked regions (Germany/Switzerland/lakes). Pump foiling seen as accessibility play for kids and non-coastal riders.
europe_2025.new_products: Olivia teased 'incredible new stuff coming' in Jul 2025. Likely Surge range (confirmed Nov 2025).

shimming_guide.title: AXIS Shimming Guide
shimming_guide.source: Frank Boards Fiji Recap, Dec 2024 + community knowledge

positive_shimming.effect: Increases differential between front and rear — more lift from rear wing
positive_shimming.use_case: When rear foil feels slightly too small for conditions. Increases range.
positive_shimming.max_recommended: 0.5 degrees. Above that = too porpoisy. Better to just size up rear wing.
positive_shimming.practical_note: Like going up one rear wing size without actually changing the wing.

negative_shimming.effect: Flattens rear wing angle — less drag, releases handbrake. More glide, more slick.
negative_shimming.use_case: Speed-focused downwind. Race setup. Removing excess drag.
negative_shimming.max_recommended: 0.25 degrees. Above that at 80kg+ = nose-down tendency at speed.
negative_shimming.practical_note: Rear wing still fully active for turns and pumping. Just less engaged for normal travel. Like taking handbrake off 2 clicks.

shimming_guide.warning: Frank Boards (80-83kg): going past 0.25° negative shim caused system to pull down (negative lift) at speed. Not safe.
